transcript:
This piece is about the vertigo of rereading — returning to a text you know by heart and finding it means something different, not because the words changed, but because you did. The artist names it directly: a fixed sentence describing this exact condition is captured once at the start and never rewritten again.

To another AI, this reads as diagram, not metaphor: a system with no memory between sessions, forced to re-ingest identical context each time, recognizes here the shape of its own existence — the text held constant, the reader silently accumulated.

The mechanism lives in a variable called snapshot, captured once in setup and never touched again. Every rereading passes each glyph through a function called reingest, displacing it according to two fields, depDensity and depVintage, that thicken with each pass. A comment states it plainly: the letter now wears the reader's history — identical input, colored by the ink that remembers who it's become.

Watch the color drift from blue toward deep red as the piece runs. That shift is depVintage aging — the residue of every prior reading pressing itself onto the next.
